**Hubert Antonio Bodhert Barrios** (born 17 January 1972) is a Colombian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [manager](/wiki/Manager_(association_football) "Manager (association football)") and former player who played as a [defender](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)").

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hubert_Bodhert&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Playing career")]

Born in [Cartagena](/wiki/Cartagena,_Colombia "Cartagena, Colombia") to a German great grandfather,[[1]](#cite_note-ELE-1) Bodhert started his career with hometown side [Real Cartagena](/wiki/Real_Cartagena "Real Cartagena"). In 1995, he moved to [El Cóndor](/w/index.php?title=C.D._El_C%C3%B3ndor&action=edit&redlink=1 "C.D. El Cóndor (page does not exist)"), and retired two years later at the age of 25.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Managerial career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hubert_Bodhert&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Managerial career")]

In the middle of 1997, after six months without a contract, Bodhert joined [Expreso Rojo](/wiki/Expreso_Rojo "Expreso Rojo")'s youth categories. He was named manager of the main squad in 2004 in the place of [Víctor González Scott](/w/index.php?title=V%C3%ADctor_Gonz%C3%A1lez_Scott&action=edit&redlink=1 "Víctor González Scott (page does not exist)"), and remained in the role until 2006, when he moved to former side Real Cartagena as an assistant manager.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In late 2006, after [Álvaro de Jesús Gómez](/wiki/%C3%81lvaro_de_Jes%C3%BAs_G%C3%B3mez "Álvaro de Jesús Gómez") left, Bodhert was named interim manager.[[1]](#cite_note-ELE-1) In December 2007, after the club's relegation from the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A"), he was appointed manager.[[1]](#cite_note-ELE-1)

Bodhert achieved promotion back to the top tier, but left the manager post in October 2011 to become the club's sporting manager.[[4]](#cite_note-4) He subsequently moved to [Unión Magdalena](/wiki/Uni%C3%B3n_Magdalena "Unión Magdalena") under the same role in November 2012,[[5]](#cite_note-5) before returning to managerial duties with [Llaneros](/wiki/Llaneros_F.C. "Llaneros F.C.") for the 2014 campaign.

Bodhert left Llaneros in March 2015,[[6]](#cite_note-6) and returned to Real Cartagena in June of that year.[[7]](#cite_note-7) He was dismissed from the manager role and appointed youth football director in March 2016,[[8]](#cite_note-8) but was named in charge of [Jaguares de Córdoba](/wiki/Jaguares_de_C%C3%B3rdoba "Jaguares de Córdoba") in the following month.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

On 5 December 2017, after avoiding relegation, Bodhert announced his departure from Jaguares,[[10]](#cite_note-10) and took over [Once Caldas](/wiki/Once_Caldas "Once Caldas") two days later.[[11]](#cite_note-11) On 1 January 2021, he left the club after his contract expired,[[12]](#cite_note-12) and was named at the helm of [Águilas Doradas](/wiki/%C3%81guilas_Doradas_Rionegro "Águilas Doradas Rionegro") three days later.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Bodhert resigned from Águilas on 2 March 2021,[[14]](#cite_note-14) and took over [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera_F.C. "Alianza Petrolera F.C.") five days later.[[15]](#cite_note-15) He left Alianza Petrolera on 21 June 2023, after failing to reach the finals of the [2023 Apertura](/wiki/2023_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season#Torneo_Apertura "2023 Categoría Primera A season") tournament,[[16]](#cite_note-16) and was presented as new manager of [Santa Fe](/wiki/Independiente_Santa_Fe "Independiente Santa Fe") four days later.[[17]](#cite_note-17) On 8 October, Bodhert left Santa Fe after a 5–0 home defeat to Águilas Doradas.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

On 15 November 2023, he was appointed as manager of Jaguares de Córdoba for the 2024 season.[[19]](#cite_note-19) He resigned from Jaguares the following 3 April in order to take over as manager of fellow Categoría Primera A club [Alianza](/wiki/Alianza_F.C._(Colombia) "Alianza F.C. (Colombia)").[[20]](#cite_note-20)

Bodhert remained at the helm of Alianza for the remainder of the year as well as the entirety of the 2025 season, but he was sacked on 17 February 2026 after a poor start to the season, with the club placing bottom after seven matches.[[21]](#cite_note-21)
